ocr.text: In alignment with the European Union's ambitious climate goals, BASF SE is committed to significantly reducing its carbon
footprint across its operations in the Chemicals and Materials sector. The transition towards a more sustainable future
necessitates comprehensive strategies aimed at mitigating greenhouse gas emissions, particularly in light of the European Green
Deal which targets a climate-neutral continent by 2050. As part of our ongoing efforts, we have established clear carbon

footprint reduction targets that underscore our commitment to sustainability and environmental stewardship. 

The accompanying
data table delineates the company's absolute emissions in metric tons of CO2 equivalent (tCO2e), providing a detailed account
of our targeted reductions over the next decade. Notably, the metrics outlined in the columns labeled "20% Reduction" and
"Scope 1" serve as critical indicators of our progress and strategic priorities. The "Scope 1" emissions, which encompass direct
emissions from owned or controlled sources, are particularly significant as they represent a fundamental area for intervention.
By focusing on reducing these emissions, BASF aims not only to comply with regulatory requirements but also to set a

benchmark for industry standards in emissions management. 

The target of reducing absolute emissions to 90,000 tCO2e by the
year 2030 reflects our commitment to a 20% reduction from baseline levels, demonstrating a proactive approach to climate
action. Achieving this target will require investment in innovative technologies, process optimizations, and enhanced energy
efficiency measures.

ReductionGoals  2020  2021  2022  Comments
AbsoluteEmissions  100,000tCO2e  95,000tCO2e  90,000tCO2e  20%Reduction  15%Reduction  10%Reduction  Scope1  60,000tCO2e
Scope2  40,000tCO2e  35,000tCO2e  30,000tCO2e  30%Reduction  25%Reduction  20%Reduction  Scope3  50,000tCO2e
55,000tCO2e  60,000tCO2e  15%Reduction  10%Increase  5%Increase  TargetAchievement  OnTrack  Delayed  Achieved

RenewableEnergy  SustainableMaterials  EnergyEfficiency  Targetsfor2023  NetZeroby2050  IntermediateGoals  40%by2025  60%by2030  Total
Furthermore, the incremental reduction targets of 15% and 10% outlined in the table are designed to facilitate a phased

approach, allowing for the integration of sustainable practices across our operations while maintaining operational integrity. 

In
conclusion, these targets not only align with BASF's sustainability goals but also reflect our dedication to contributing to the
global effort of combating climate change. The data presented in the table serves as a roadmap for our emissions reduction
strategy, illustrating our commitment to transparency and accountability as we work towards a more sustainable future for the
Chemicals and Materials industry. Through continued innovation and collaboration, BASF SE is poised to lead the way in
reducing carbon emissions while delivering value to our stakeholders and society at large.
